text: Detecting small targets in remote sensing imagery remains a significant challenge due to heavy background noise and the scarcity of pixel-level information, often leading to suboptimal performance in standard detection frameworks. To address these limitations, we propose LAR-YOLOv8, an enhanced detection algorithm designed to improve localization and classification accuracy for small-scale objects. Our approach introduces three key innovations: First, we strengthen the feature extraction network by integrating a dual-branch attention architecture with a vision transformer block, maximizing the expressive power of the feature maps. Second, we develop an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (BiFPN) that utilizes a dynamic sparse attention mechanism to capture discriminative details from shallow layers, while incorporating top-down paths to facilitate more effective feature fusion. Finally, we introduce the RIOU loss function to prevent gradient instability and ensure better shape alignment between predicted and ground-truth bounding boxes. Quantitative evaluations on the NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves superior performance in terms of mAP (small), overall mAP, and inference speed, confirming the efficacy of our architectural modifications over the baseline YOLOv8 model.
